samples.text: A number can be both greater than two and less than ten. Is this logically consistent?
A number is simultaneously larger than two and smaller than ten. Does this make logical sense?
A value can be both above two and below ten. Is this logically consistent?
The ranges overlap between two and ten. A number like five satisfies both, so this is consistent. Is the described situation logically consistent?
A number can be both greater than two and less than ten. The meeting started at noon. Is this logically consistent?
